How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-06-30 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers appearing across 2+ coherent screeners within one trading day, ranking validated combo matches highest to exploit signal stacking.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AMAT leads at 314 on BREAKOUT plus CLENOW_MOMENTUM plus VOLUME_LEADER, the cleanest momentum-confirming stack in the batch. SLS prints 247 via BREAKOUT, PULLBACK, and SMART_MONEY_SURGE, which is an interesting tension between a fresh break and a dip signal that warrants checking the intraday structure before sizing. CAT hits 4 screeners at 160, the widest breadth of any name today, though no ticker in this batch matches a validated combo (all matched_combo fields are null), which is the single biggest flag against acting aggressively. Score distribution is top-heavy: AMAT and SLS sit well above a cluster of 95-160, and the macro regime is NEUTRAL at 53.95, not a tape that rewards high-conviction punts. First preference is AMAT at low conviction, given the macro read and the absence of any VALID combo confirmation.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: VRTX (score 5, target ~$475.82 (DD20d -4.3%)).

2 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

🧠 Identifies institutional options sweeps with next-day OI confirmation and EMA trend alignment to flag directional smart-money positioning before the crowd.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $TLT
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1TLT ↑60 Watch$85.48 -1.09%BULLISH SWEEPCALL75$605KNOT_FOUND—94STRONG88
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record posts 100% win rate on just 5 resolved trades out of 290, which is statistically empty, and the April and May monthly cohorts mirror this with 100% win rates and four-digit average returns, so the only credible performance anchor remains the backtest Sharpe of 2.39 across 273 trades.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 290 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single name: TLT at score 60, Watch tier, flagging a bullish sweep in long-duration Treasuries. Nothing clears 65 and nothing clears 80, so the screener produced one borderline signal against a NEUTRAL macro backdrop with a regime score of 53.95, which does not actively support duration longs. Score distribution is as thin as it gets, with median and max both pinned at 60. If you act at all, TLT is the only option and conviction is low given the score floor, the neutral tape, and the complete absence of confirmatory names.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 62.8%, avg +3.4% vs SPY -0.9%, verdict KEEP) and May 2026 cohort (WR 61.5%, avg +5.3%) both confirm the backtest Sharpe of 2.34 holds in live conditions, with the +4.3-point SPY spread being the number that keeps this screener credible.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NEE leads at 77 and four other utilities, PCG, D, SO, and EXC, fill out the top eight, which fits directly into Sinclair's +31% structural edge for utility premium selling. KMB at 76 is the lone consumer staples name worth watching alongside the utility cluster. The score distribution is compressed: all 41 signals land between 65 and 77, median 68, zero names clear 80, so this is a mid-conviction clustered batch with no standout outlier. NEUTRAL macro at 53.95 neither helps nor hurts the premium-selling thesis, but it does mean the environment is not actively supportive. Lead with NEE at modest conviction, understanding that the absence of any 80-plus score caps the urgency of the setup.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

PATH at 100 and BBY at 99 are the standout names, both Very Strong tier, though pattern context is null for every pick so the scores reflect VRP positioning alone. MARA at 97 is the third candidate, and its pairing with RIOT at 94 means crypto miners hold two of the five highest scores - treat that as a sector cluster, not independent conviction. The distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals clear 80 and the median lands at 75, with 22 of 30 above 65, which is a well-populated, high-quality batch. A NEUTRAL macro at 53.95 provides no directional tailwind but no structural headwind either, so the screener is running on its own merits. First preference is PATH at high conviction given the maximum score; skip MARA and RIOT as a pair unless you want deliberate crypto vol exposure.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals score exactly 100 with every name landing in the Very Strong tier, which means the screener is ceiling-pinned and offering zero internal discrimination today. Among the named picks, APD and CPRT stand out purely on liquidity and institutional visibility grounds, both flagging LONG with perfect scores, but pattern fields are null across the board so the specific put-vol structure driving each signal is unverifiable. A batch where median equals max equals 50-of-50 at the ceiling is a warning sign, not a green light - it more likely reflects a scoring artifact than 50 simultaneous clean setups. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 53.95, which neither supports nor kills the contrarian-hedge thesis, but the undifferentiated pile makes any single conviction call difficult. If forced to act, APD first at low size given sector stability, but treat this batch as thin on signal quality until the distribution spreads.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape (WR 67%, avg +4.8%, beating a -2.0% SPY by 6.8 points) confirms the backtest's 65% win rate, but the April and May monthly cohorts running at 53-57% WR and under +2.6% avg reveal a softening trend that the aggregate 90d figure masks.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 67% · avg +4.8% · beats SPY -2.0% by +6.8 pts
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 495 resolved at +20d · WR 67% · avg +4.8% · vs SPY -2.0%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 500 closed · WR TB 43% · 16% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

U scores a perfect 100 with the LAUNCH pattern and leads the batch cleanly; PTON at 95 is the only other name above 90 before eight tickers crowd the 90 tier, including ASYS and NVCR. Score distribution is top-heavy by construction: all 50 signals clear 80, median sits at 85, so there is no weak tail but also no meaningful dispersion to sort quality from noise. NEUTRAL macro at 53.95 does not block pullback reversals but removes any wind-at-back assumption for fast mean-reversion. One structural note: the 90d tracking shows the Strong tier (avg +6.3%) has materially outrun Very strong (avg +2.7%), which argues for treating ASYS or ENS at 90 as real alternatives to U, not just filler. Start with U at moderate conviction given the perfect score, add ASYS as the secondary if you want tier diversification.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

LI tops the batch at 94 (Very strong), the only name that separates from the pack, with BTU at 89 and AD at 88 as the next-best reads. Pattern data is null across all eight names, so there is no secondary setup signal to lean on. The score distribution is top-heavy and compressed - all 25 signals land at 80 or above with a median of 84, which means there is essentially no bottom tier pulling down the batch, but also little internal spread to distinguish the field. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 53.95) is neither a tailwind nor a friction point for a Stage 4 contrarian basket. LI is the first preference, basket-sized at 1-2% per name via LEAPS held to the 120-day window, conviction moderate given the absence of pattern confirmation.

🧠 Detects vol-of-vol climax via VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, and FOMC proximity to flag short-volatility entry points.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑61 Watch$742.67 -0.55%SELL VOL1.33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Detects institutional options prints exceeding 5x 20-day average volume with a $50K premium floor and calm-tape overlay, betting heavy directional flow precedes price movement.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikeDTEPremiumVol/OIIVDeltaTimingFlowOIToxicity

2KWEB ↑58 Watch$24.71 +1.00%BULLISH SWEEPCALL2629$1864K342.7038.1%0.34EARLY850.54
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape shows 100% win rate on 24 resolved signals with avg +189.6% versus SPY -0.55%, which confirms the KEEP verdict, but 24 resolved out of 620 total is a dangerously thin resolved base and does not override the backtest's MARGINAL flag at p_bonferroni of 0.10.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SMH leads at score 84 (Strong) but carries a BEARISH SWEEP pattern against a LONG direction tag - a direct conflict that demands caution before acting on the long side. QQQ (75, Signal) and SOXX (74, Signal) repeat the same contradiction, meaning three of the top four names show smart money buying puts on semis while the system outputs LONG, which is the real signal here - not a buy setup. SPY (72) and FITB (69) offer cleaner BULLISH SWEEP reads, but SPY is the benchmark and FITB is a regional bank with no obvious catalyst in the data provided. Score distribution is top-heavy with one name above 80, five above 65, and a median of 58, so the lower half of the 11-signal batch is Watch-tier noise. NEUTRAL macro (53.95) is neither an obstacle nor a tailwind. Preference is to treat the semi bearish sweeps (SMH first) as a directional put thesis, low conviction, rather than chasing the LONG tag.

Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLC and XLE are the only two names printing at the maximum score of 80 (Strong tier), making them the clear leaders in today's batch. XLV is the sole Signal-tier name at 65, a modest step below the top two but still above the median. The remaining five names - XLF, XLRE, XLK, XLI, XLU - cluster at Watch (50 to 55), dragging the median to 55 and leaving the distribution sharply bifurcated rather than broadly constructive. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 53.95 provides no directional lift and does not argue for pressing momentum exposure broadly. With no pattern classification available for any name, take XLC first at moderate conviction given the score gap separating it from the Watch cluster, and hold off on the Watch names until the macro regime firms.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape flatly contradicts the backtest: 43% WR and avg -0.4% vs the backtest's 63.3% win rate, and while monthly cohorts recovered from January's -9.5% average to March's 60% WR, May's slip back to 45.8% confirms the edge is not reproducing live, with the 20-point WR gap being the key number.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks stocks by 90-day exponential-regression slope times R-squared, buying only above the 100-day MA and only when SPY holds its 200-day MA.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian LONG on SPY when two or more intermarket correlation pairs snap beyond 2-sigma over seven days, treating dislocation as mean-reversion fuel.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Detects stocks holding a post-earnings gap-up on positive EPS surprise, capturing analyst-revision-driven price continuation over 1-3 weeks.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BB ↑95●●● Very strong$12.92 +2.13%50.0%420.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape at 61% WR and +4.4% avg against a SPY return of -0.02% confirms the live signal is producing real edge above the MARGINAL backtest label, and the May cohort (n=709, 59.8% WR, +3.9% avg) is directionally consistent, though the Very strong tier's 64.5% WR and +5.1% avg is the key number that most directly benchmarks today's sole BB entry.

Today's batch is as thin as it gets: one name, BB, scoring 95 and sitting in the Very strong tier with a PRIMED pattern. The macro regime prints NEUTRAL at 53.95, neither a headwind nor a tailwind for drift plays. BB is the only actionable name by definition, so it goes first, but the one-stock batch and a marginal-validity backtest cap conviction at moderate; size accordingly and do not treat the high score as a crowded-thesis confirmation when there is nothing else in the queue to compare it to.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape confirms the KILL: overall WR of 49% and avg return of -1.1% beat SPY by just 0.6 points, and the only sub-tier generating positive alpha is Very strong (WR 54.8%, avg +0.85%), while Strong (-2.6%) and Signal (-2.1%) drag the composite into negative territory, with a time-boxed WR of just 28.8% underscoring that most winners expire before the 40-day window closes.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is a red flag before looking at any name: all 50 signals print at exactly 100, median 100, every signal above 80, meaning the screener offers zero ranking signal today and names must be judged on fundamentals alone. AMZN and APP are the only names worth touching on liquidity grounds, both Very strong tier at 100; the rest of the visible picks (ABVX, ACNT, AFK, AMPX, ASM, ARKW) are thin or illiquid vehicles that inflate the count without adding actionable substance. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 53.95, which is not a tailwind for a momentum cross. With a live KILL verdict on the screener, conviction is low across the board; if forced to act, AMZN first at minimal size, and only if it holds structure on the daily.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms real edge at 58.2% win rate and +4.3% average return beating SPY by 6.2 points, and the May 2026 cohort recovering to 56.9% WR and +4.0% average from April's weak 44.4% WR and -0.73% average supports the KEEP verdict, though the options track at only 35% barrier win rate with 24% of contracts expiring worthless flags that binary timing risk is the primary way this trade kills you.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ACHC prints a perfect score of 100 in the Very strong tier with the PRIMED pattern, joined by BEAM and BV at 95, making them the headline names today. The critical problem is that Very strong tier is the worst historical performer in this screener at a 45.8% win rate and -1.53% average 20-day return, a hard inversion relative to Watch tier's 62.2% win rate and +5.70% average. Score distribution is top-heavy and compressed: all 50 signals clear 65, the median is 75, and 18 breach 80, meaning there is almost no spread to differentiate on score alone. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (53.95), neither accelerating nor killing squeeze setups. Preference is ABX or APGE at score 85 in the Strong tier, at low conviction, before touching the Very strong cluster where history says the edge collapses.

🧠 Ranks RUT-1000 stocks by relative volume across three lookback windows multiplied by multi-horizon price momentum, surfacing names where volume leadership and price strength simultaneously peak.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

AMAT and KLAC (scores 100 and 99, Very strong) anchor a dense semiconductor-equipment cluster at the top, while MRNA at 100 is the lone biotech name showing anomalous volume leadership into the close. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 53.95 provides no directional help. The critical problem is that Very strong - the tier every name here carries - is the worst-performing bucket in live tracking at a 46.9% win rate and -2.67% average 20-day return, so this batch is weak; if forced to touch one name, AMAT has the most liquid options market of the semis for expressing a short-term momentum trade, but conviction is low and size should reflect that.

🧠 Detects large OTM block trades on quiet stocks, confirmed by next-day OI delta to filter new positions from closing flow.
Construction
Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape actively contradicts the screener, posting a 43% win rate and avg return of -4.2% vs SPY -2.1% across 490 resolved trades for a KILL verdict, but the April and May 2026 monthly cohorts both flipped positive at +8.8% and +2.5% avg with win rates above 52%, suggesting the 90d drag is rear-loaded and the key number to watch is whether the June cohort sustains that recovery or reverts.

CWAN is the only name worth isolating today, scoring 93 as the sole Very strong entry in a batch of 43. HOMZ at 85 and SMQ at 82 are the next tier down, both Strong, but pattern data is null across every pick, leaving score and premium weight as the only differentiators. The distribution is modestly top-heavy: 8 names above 80, 25 above 65, and a median of 67, meaning the majority of today's batch is mid-grade filler. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (53.95), offering no directional lift. One caution: the 90d tier data shows Very strong names actually underperform Watch names badly, so CWAN's top score is not a free pass. If forced to act, CWAN first at minimal size, with the screener's KILL verdict as a hard ceiling on conviction.

🧠 Catches short-side excess highs: rejection tail at least 1.8x ATR with price at or above the 70th percentile of the value area.
Construction
Mirror of DALTON: excess high bars at the top of the value area, rejection tail >= 1.8x ATR, location >= 70%.
Top picks: $JSML $CEPO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %
1JSML ↓100●●● Very strong$92.77 -0.31%HIGH2.4778.4%

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) in the last 3 bars
  • SHORT direction: excess on the HIGH side AND trade location ≥ 70%
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). SHORT.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects mean-reversion entries using Market Profile rejection tails at value-area extremes, where auction theory predicts price failure and reversal.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $GLRE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1GLRE ↑100●●● Very strong$16.30 +0.74%LOW2.0928.5%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Three resolved signals is nowhere near the 20 needed for a verdict, so the 66.67% win rate and +3.85% avg at 20 days (vs a near-zero SPY baseline of 0.056%) are directionally consistent with the backtest's +3.18% at 10 days but carry no statistical weight, and the tape-based signal tracker at 37.5% win rate on 8 trades actively contradicts both, making the key number to watch the TB win rate as more signals resolve.

Data confidence STARTER PEAD gap-up + weekly trigger, small N, mechanically sound but too early for a verdict Full methodology →
🧠 Post-earnings drift continuation entry: weekly red-candle pullback inside 4 weeks of a gap-up, triggered on a weekly close above the prior high.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.
Top picks: $AOUT
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayGapVol ratioBars ago
1AOUT ↑88●● Strong$12.63 +7.58%5.9%2.631
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%
  • Volume confirmation: gap-day volume / 20d average > 1.5
  • Dollar-volume ≥ $50M
Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.

🧠 Filters SMART_FLOW institutional signals by blocking entries when KVO or Force Index flip within three days, using volume conflict as a negative screen to isolate cleaner flow.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).

  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

TLT is the only signal today, scoring 60 at Watch tier on a BULLISH SWEEP pattern - the lowest passing grade this screener produces. Score distribution is not a distribution: one name, nothing at or above 65, nothing at or above 80. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 53.95 neither supports nor actively blocks a rates long, but one Watch-tier name in a flat-regime tape is a thin batch by any measure. If pressing it, TLT first and only, at reduced conviction given the score and the absence of any confirming names.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape (47.8% WR, +1.06% avg versus SPY at -0.84%) delivers a thin +1.9-point edge that is almost entirely explained by the Strong tier's +15.1% average, and while April and May monthly cohorts show improving average returns of +12.7% and +4.6% respectively, none of that rescues the backtest REJECT anchored by a Bonferroni p-value of 1.0 on just 55 trades, so the edge is suggestive at best and structurally unconfirmed.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AGL is the only name worth serious attention, scoring a perfect 100 in the Very strong tier with no pattern label, so the score itself is doing the work. INTW prints 75 but lands in the Signal tier, which in live 90-day tracking carries a negative average return of -4.09% despite a 55.8% win rate, making it a yield trap. The remaining five names, SOXL, AMDL, MVLL, ANRO, SLS, HYMC, all cluster in Watch with scores between 36 and 42, and that tier has averaged -10.35% over 90 days, so they are background noise. Score distribution is severely bottom-heavy: median 23, only 2 signals clearing 65 out of 37 total, a weak batch by any measure. NEUTRAL macro at 53.95 provides no wind. AGL first, half-conviction, no chasing given the absent pattern confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 55%, avg +1.1%, beats SPY by 2.6 pts) justifies the KEEP verdict, but the tier inversion - Strong at -8.4% average while Watch delivers +1.3% - is the key number that undermines any confidence in the highest-scored names, and the monthly win-rate slide from 68.5% in March to 54.4% in May confirms the edge is narrowing, not widening.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BTQ, DFTX, and NVCT all score 80 with TRIGGER patterns, but the 90-day tier data runs directly against them: the Strong tier (n=7) posts a 42.9% win rate and -8.4% average return, the worst of any tier by a wide margin. The 75-score Signal names - ARQQ, BLZE, GEO, GFS, SMWB - sit in a tier tracking +1.0% average, which is where the live edge actually lives. Score distribution is compressed and top-heavy: 44 of 50 signals land at 65 or above, 3 hit the 80 ceiling, suggesting the scoring model is not separating names today so much as clustering them. Macro is NEUTRAL at 53.95, no directional tailwind for the short thesis. If forced to act, favor the Signal-tier names over the 80-score TRIGGER trio at low conviction, and size small given the backtest rejection.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Every one of the 50 signals scores 99 or 100 - the model is pegged at the ceiling, which destroys cross-sectional differentiation and is itself a warning sign. Within that undifferentiated pile, STX, WDC, and LRCX stand out as a coherent storage-and-semis cluster that at least tells a consistent sector story; AMD and AMAT layer in logic-side semis if you want breadth. Pattern data is null across the board, so there is no additional signal to lean on. The score distribution is not top-heavy, it is flatlined at the top, which means rank ordering within this batch is meaningless. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (53.95), neither a tailwind nor a headwind. Given the REJECT verdict on the backtest (alpha -0.8%/yr, p=0.25, headline return was a leveraged-ETF artifact), conviction here is low across the board; if forced to act, STX as the highest-momentum storage name is the first call, but size it as a watch-list touch, not a full position.

🧠 Flags extreme weekly RSI oversold stocks (RSI14 below 30) then scores daily reversal confirmation via RSI crossover, candlestick pattern, and demethod zone overlap.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $DKL $BORR $FANG

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Detects unusual institutional flow in crypto-linked ETFs via volume-vs-mean, premium percentage, and OI delta, weighted by spot crypto correlation.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $CORZ $HUT $MARA

3MARA ↑80●● Strong$13.44 -3.24%54.3711.3%neutral
4RIOT ↑50 Watch$25.55 -6.68%61.0514.7%neutral
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The KEEP verdict rests almost entirely on Watch-tier names (n=70, 64% WR, +2.6% avg), while the Strong and Signal tiers that produce today's top picks have delivered -13.2% and -20.2% avg respectively, a tier inversion that flatly contradicts using score rank as a conviction proxy.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

HUT leads at score 95 with a TRIGGER pattern, followed by CORZ at 85 and CLSK at 80, all three printing the highest-conviction TRIGGER signal in the Very Strong and Strong tiers. The score distribution is top-heavy: three names at 80-plus, median 75, then a cliff to 55 and two 50s. NEUTRAL macro (53.95) neither supports nor opposes a crypto-flow trade today. The critical problem is the 90-day tier breakdown: Strong names resolved at 0% win rate with a -13.2% avg, and Signal names at 12.5% win rate with -20.2% avg, meaning the three highest-scoring picks today sit in exactly the tiers that have destroyed capital in live tape. If you must act, preference CLSK at 80 over HUT and CORZ only because it is at the lower end of the damaged Strong tier, and size it small given the historical tier inversion.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ARKG and IFRA share the top score of 78, both Signal-tier longs with no pattern data attached, making them the lead names by score alone. VHT at 75 and XLV at 73 pull healthcare into a batch that leans defensive rather than genuinely thematic, which is a yellow flag for a screener built around emerging categories. The distribution runs from a median of 60 to a ceiling of 78, with zero names above 80 and only 12 of 50 clearing 65 - a middling, not top-heavy batch that signals thin momentum across the theme universe today. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 53.95 provides no directional lift for speculative theme positioning, and the healthcare clustering makes this batch look more like a sector rotation signal than an emerging-theme call. ARKG first at low conviction for its genomics optionality and highest score, but do not size aggressively into a batch this soft.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names hit a perfect score of 100 in the Very strong tier today - BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM - though no pattern data is available for any, which limits differentiation within the group. SRTY is a 3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF flagged LONG, sitting awkwardly inside a long-only batch against a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 53.95), and its presence raises a question about whether the screener is capturing mean-reversion candidates rather than pure momentum names. The score distribution is top-heavy: 12 of 50 signals at 80-plus, 41 of 50 at 65-plus, and a median of 70, meaning today's batch clusters hard at the ceiling with little gradation to help rank order. With no pattern tags and the macro backdrop offering no tailwind, treat the batch as low-conviction; if forced to act, take BL first as a clean equity name without the ETF complexity of SRTY or the sector noise of CELH.

Construction

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ASML at 97 and FB at 93 are the only two names clearing the Very Strong tier, meaning multiple funds simultaneously opened new positions or aggressively added, which is the ceiling signal in this scoring system. AVGO at 79 is the next name down, sitting just below the 80 threshold and still worth attention given semis have consistent representation across the top of the list alongside TSM and MU. The score distribution is top-heavy and thin: only 2 names above 80, 13 above 65, and a median of 55 out of 50 signals, so the bulk of the batch is low-conviction noise. NEUTRAL macro at 53.95 provides no directional tailwind for this style, and the structural 60-to-90-day stale lag on 13F data means whale entries here reflect Q1 portfolio moves, not current positioning. First preference is ASML, high conviction, but check whether the stock has already repriced since the filing period before sizing.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Three names tie at the top score of 100 - SVRE, JEF, and COE - all graded Very Strong, though none carry a pattern tag, so the thesis rests entirely on the dollar-value and rank weighting with no price-action overlay. JEF is the only institutional-scale, liquid name in that trio, which makes it the most actionable for a meaningful position size. The score distribution is almost useless as a filter: all 46 signals land at 80 or above, median 94, meaning the screener is producing a single compressed cluster rather than a ranked stack, and differentiation within the batch is minimal. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 53.95 neither confirms nor blocks insider-buy setups, but it removes any macro tailwind. Take JEF first at low-to-moderate conviction, sized conservatively until a pattern or additional fundamental context fills in the blank pattern fields.

🧠 Ranks US equities by EV/EBIT ascending, surfacing the cheapest operating-earnings names as potential mean-reversion longs.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 There is nothing to confirm or contradict: zero of 37 tracked trades have resolved, the screener has no completed backtest, and the only available number - a tentative win rate of 40.0% across the 10-trade thumbnail bucket - is too thin to treat as signal rather than noise.

🧠 Detects stocks with 3+ insiders buying within 30 days via Form 4, weighted by rank and dollar size, to exploit coordinated accumulation signals.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 Finds individual stocks lagging their sector move by scoring sector relative strength against stock under-performance within LATE_CHASE and RIDING sectors.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags parabolic retail-driven moves with no earnings catalyst by combining social attention z-score, call/put ratio, and price stretch to isolate crowded names ripe for mean reversion.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $AMD $BXP $ARE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1AMD ↓100●●● Very strong$553.80 -4.67%FOMO1.123.4%
2BXP ↓99●●● Very strong$66.19 -0.18%EUPHORIA0.99-1.9%
3ARE ↓89●● Strong$53.10 +0.48%EUPHORIA0.89-2.9%
4BKNG ↓78● Signal$180.46 +1.25%EUPHORIA0.78-1.3%
5ANET ↓68● Signal$162.88 -4.12%GREED0.68-0.1%
6CCJ ↓66● Signal$101.15 -0.70%GREED0.66-2.0%
7CART ↓55 Watch$42.66 -9.90%GREED0.551.3%
8CAT ↓55 Watch$1017.44 -4.46%GREED0.550.8%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape confirms the screener is working, 60% WR and +0.61% average beats SPY at -0.48% by 1.1 points, and the May cohort (n=88, 62.5% WR, +1.79% avg) reinforces that trend, but the Signal tier's -7.0% average on 16 resolved trades is the single number that argues against acting on today's BKNG, ANET, and CCJ despite their scores clearing 65.

🧠 Ranks top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors using 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster position to surface rotation leaders.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $AAT $AAMI $ABCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$24.84 +0.61%Real Estate19.0%31.5%
2AAMI ↑76● Signal$71.61 +0.13%Financials9.2%52.1%
3ABCL ↑74● Signal$7.71 -1.78%Healthcare7.8%47.3%
4A ↑65● Signal$133.78 +0.72%Healthcare15.9%17.1%
5ABCB ↑56 Watch$89.42 -0.94%Financials4.9%15.6%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape supports keeping this screener - 55% WR and +4.1% avg versus SPY at -1.8% is a +5.8-point spread on 176 resolved trades - but a single monthly cohort means no trend across cohorts exists yet to confirm or contradict that number.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects bear and bull traps by flagging a false break of value area low or high followed by a next-day reclaim, trapping trapped-side participants.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $ARCC $ARLO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1ARCC ↑73● Signal$18.72 +1.03%BEAR_TRAP18.1418.730.32
2ARLO ↑65● Signal$13.69 +1.56%BEAR_TRAP12.1712.940.60
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (17 resolved, need 20 for a clean WATCH) shows a 70.6% win rate averaging +4.80% vs SPY at -1.14%, and May's cohort adds 76.9%/+7.97%, which is directionally supportive, but the time-based count of 47 trades carries only a 51.1% win rate - the key number that warns against over-reading the early resolved sample.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Identifies stocks where put IV crushes call IV above the 90th percentile, flagging unusual bearish hedging or directional put demand.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$370.73 -1.86%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Seven resolved trades show a 71.4% win rate, but the average 20d return of -0.33% trails SPY's +1.43% baseline by 177 basis points, and the April cohort's -88.77% outlier in the Strong tier confirms the screener's worst-case tail is severe enough to swamp otherwise decent win rates - the verdict stays WATCH until at least 20 resolved trades are in hand.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle IV underprices realized move expectation derived from Yang-Zhang HV20, targeting vol cheapness into the event.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $MU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1MU ↓50 Watch$1068.47 -7.43%REVERSAL1DOWN (reversal)BUY PUT (reversal)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is weak: one signal, MU at a score of 50 with a Watch tier and no pattern identified, which is the floor of the scoring range. Every threshold above 65 has zero names, and the single-name distribution is as thin as it gets. A SHORT direction on MU with no confirming pattern and a score that barely qualifies is not a high-conviction setup. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (53.95), which does not add or subtract meaningfully. Pass on MU until the score climbs above 65 or a pattern populates.

This batch is weak - there is no actionable name, and BSX at Watch conviction does not justify a position.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where IV30 minus HV20 exceeds 1.5 standard deviations on a 90-day window, identifying inflated fear premium as a candidate for options selling.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $NFLX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1NFLX ↓62 Watch$73.16 +2.46%46%30%1.55FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (45% WR, avg +2.3% vs SPY +1.3%, WATCH verdict) offers a slim live edge, but the monthly cohort trend partially contradicts that read - April posted a strong +10.8% avg on 68 signals while May deteriorated sharply to -0.22% avg on 212 signals, suggesting the edge is regime-dependent and fading, with no validated backtest to confirm whether any of it is structural.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects 25-delta put/call skew spikes vs 90-day baseline, reading extreme bearish hedging demand as a contrarian signal when underlying price action is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $TSLA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1TSLA ↓100●●● Very strong$423.46 +0.68%133%87—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 54%, avg +9.4%, beats SPY by 8.1 pts) and the May and April monthly cohorts (avg +10.1% and +9.2%) are consistent with each other and support keeping the screener, but the key number is the tier inversion - Watch posts WR 65.9% and avg +15.5% while Very strong delivers WR 16.7% and avg -5.1%, meaning score rank is currently an inverse predictor of outcome, which contradicts any naive interpretation of the tier labels.

Exact criteria & score formula

EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens US equities using Altman Z-score financial distress signals to surface potential credit stress or recovery candidates.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is empty. One signal fired but scored zero, placing it below every meaningful threshold, with no names reaching 65 or 80. There are no candidates worth naming. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL with a score of 53.95, which offers no tailwind to justify lowering the bar. Pass on today entirely.

Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

🧠 VSA identifies volume spread analysis patterns where price range and volume diverge from expected behavior, signaling institutional absorption or distribution.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

A NEUTRAL macro regime at 53.95 would ordinarily support selective long setups, but there is nothing here to act on. Conviction is zero; pass on the screener entirely today.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
